definitions: One field in a table — like 'name' or 'email'
A separate system that stores and manages data — accessed through backend code
Not the backend itself — it is managed by the backend. Together, they form the backend system.
A unique ID for each row in a table — no two rows can have the same primary key
A question you ask the database — used to read, write, update, or delete data
The structure of your tables — defines what columns exist and what type of data each holds
A collection of similar data — like a spreadsheet with rows and columns
